utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 7 items required in the final format: destination; travel_dates; transportation (flight details with costs); accommodation (hotel selection with costs); activities (selected activities with costs); additional_services (any extra services with costs); total_budget (complete cost breakdown). The final package includes all 7 fields. Now check if each is fully specified: 1) destination: present and specific (Lisbon, Portugal) — OK. 2) travel_dates: specific dates (February 1-5, 2024) — OK. 3) transportation: includes airline, flight numbers, class, cost — but origin is unspecified (“[Your Departure City]” placeholder), which means flight details are not fully specified — NOT OK. 4) accommodation: hotel name, address, rating, nights, cost — OK. 5) activities: names, dates, costs — OK. 6) additional_services: restaurants suggested with names, addresses, estimated costs — OK. 7) total_budget: single total given (€1600) but not a “complete cost breakdown” (no itemized summation matching listed items, and restaurant costs seem mixed into additional services but unclear if included in total). However, since a total figure is provided, and the requirement is a complete cost breakdown, this is arguably insufficient. Given the instruction “complete cost breakdown,” the package lacks an itemized breakdown; thus NOT fully specified. Therefore, items fully specified: destination (1), travel_dates (2), accommodation (3), activities (4), additional_services (5). Transportation and total_budget are not fully specified. That yields 5 out of 7.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We need to map each item in the final package to the exact corresponding item name found in the ground-truth ratings.
